name: Sesame Beef with Asparagus
recipeIngredient: 3 large cloves garlic, crushed plus 2 tablespoons chopped
2 tablespoons Shao Hsing (Chinese rice wine) or dry sherry
2 teaspoons Chinese dark vinegar or balsamic vinegar
1 teaspoon Chinese sesame paste (available in Asian groceries)
1/2 small red bell pepper, very thinly sliced (about 1/3 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the steak into broad strips measuring approximately 1/8-inch thick, about 1 inch wide, and 6 inches long.
2. In a small bowl, combine the steak strips with baking soda, crushed garlic cloves, and 1/4 cup cold water. Mix well and soak for 5 hours.
3. Rinse the steak slices and dry them thoroughly on paper towels.
4. In another small bowl, mix together the egg white and salt, stirring vigorously until the egg white foams. Stir in sesame seeds, 1 teaspoon sugar, 1 teaspoon peanut oil, and cornstarch. Add the steak and toss to coat. Marinate for 30 minutes at room temperature.
5. In a separate small bowl, combine thin soy sauce, Shao Hsing (Chinese rice wine) or dry sherry, remaining 3/4 teaspoon sugar, vinegar, sesame oil, and sesame paste. Set aside.
6. Heat the remaining 4 cups peanut oil in a wok over high heat to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
7. Drain the beef, pat it dry, and deep-fry for 45 seconds, turning pieces several times with a metal spoon. Using a slotted spoon, remove the beef and drain on paper towels.
8. When the oil returns to 375 degrees Fahrenheit, deep-fry the beef another 15 seconds more, then remove and drain again on clean paper towels.
9. Drain all but 1 tablespoon of oil from the wok and increase the heat to high. Add chili peppers, scallions, chopped garlic, and chopped ginger; stir-fry for 1 minute.
10. Add asparagus and red pepper; stir-fry for 10 seconds, then add the beef. Stir in the soy sauce mixture, bring to a boil, and serve immediately with rice.
Yield: 3 to 4 servings as part of a Chinese meal
